entry.title: Re: SAP VIM PDF Invoice
entry.content: Thanks, That works Hakanhaslaman

entry.title: Re: How to synchronize Set Controlling Hierarchies in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I would like to add my experience with profit center hierarchies replication, it seems that manual replication:

- deletes old time dependancies for standard hierarchy and only actual hierarchy works in fiori reports

- does not delete time dependancies for other than standard hierarchies - report works and displays data also for hierarchy with older time dependancy. 

Kind regards

Martina

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Changes to ChaRM Phase Cycle Z_IJ5_P...
entry.content:
Hello Srinivas V, 

I am not sure if I understand your current transport tracks

Maintenance: KL6 CD2 EF3 GH4 IJ5 AB1
Project: KL6 MN7 IJ5 AB1

HANA DB migration IJ5
HANA DB migration Done in AB1, CD2

TMS Final: KL6 EF3 GH4

What I can say is that what you define at TMS level for the final transport routes is the reality and what you define in the Logical component group cannot go againts the TMS reality... After making the changes at TMS level you will need to redefine the two cycles.

Hope this helps, 

Dolores

entry.title: ‌🇮🇪‌ Irish HR & Payroll Focus Group Meeting - Dublin
entry.content:
We’re excited to invite you to the upcoming meeting on Irish HR & Payroll, hosted by the UK & Ireland SAP User Group (UKISUG)! 

SAP expert Bianca Ball will be presenting an SAP legislative update:
- pension auto enrolment
- EU Pay Directive
- SEPA file format changes
- single pension scheme for Public Sector
- SAP enhancements to gender pay gap reporting
- 2026 budget preparation
- B2B Revenue reporting requirements

Empower yourself with the expertise and strategies to effectively manage payroll, HR, and compliance challenges!

This event is targeted at members of UKISUG.

Learn more about the event here.

If you want to get more information about the Irish Payroll topics provided by SAP experts, join the UKI Finance and HCM Localisation Work Zone group.

We are currently in the process of configuring an OAuth mail destination for IAG. While the setup functions correctly with basic authentication, we are not receiving email notifications when using OAuth2 client credentials.

Is your issue resolved? Can you pls share how?

Thanks,

Namrata

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Changes to ChaRM Phase Cycle Z_IJ5_P...
entry.content: Hello @DoloresCorrea

entry.title: Training and Event Management: Attendee list Not creating
entry.content:
Dear Professional,

I have completed the configuration and all the things required for Training module, but when i am trying to book/create the attendee list via PV07. it takes around 5 minutes to buffer, and gives me ABAP runtime error. I think i am doing the right process:

Its urgent.

Thanks,

Marium khan

 

HCM (Human Capital Management)

entry.title: VIM Taking other Business partner when invoice comes with same amount and reference num but
entry.content:
Vendor sending same Invoice with same $ and Reference repeatedly,

Invoices are coming twice into VIM as system doesn’t flag it as duplicate because,

Main BP
171520868

It has Partners in the same BP
1716202376
1717293934

1st Invoice coming with same amount and Reference and processing for main BP 171520868,
Again, d same invoice is coming and processing with 1716202376, it’s not suspecting it as duplicate.

We do not have, /OTX/PF_BC_VNDR_IDENT or /OTX/PF_VNDR_RULES configurations in SAP VIM 16.3 BC, also don't have BC sets for this vendor thing to activate.

invoices come through Gmail into SAP from vendor.


How can we overcome this please help in detail.

 

Thanks In Advance.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Changes to ChaRM Phase Cycle Z_IJ5_P...
entry.content: Hello @DoloresCorrea, I have checked with BASIS Team. They were using only the Project Phase Cycle Z_IJ5_P and they have adjusted the Transport routes at Tcode STMS in the IJ5 domain controller. The phase cycle Z_IJ5_P notified me to redefine the landscape and the scope was auto adjusted as per the transport routes created by the BASIS Team. My Query is resolved.

entry.title: Re: Clarification on Changes to ChaRM Phase Cycle Z_IJ5_P...
entry.content:
perfect!

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning integration between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere - product FAQ
entry.content:
@ferdwich @erdwich,

if do we create a HANA Cloud connection to the schema managed by SAC ?

... but with what database user? as the schema and user is auto-managed and we can not grant SELECT to share the data to other database users...

Probably with database analysis user we can read the data with a replication flows (only in Initial mode as the primary keys problem is not solved). => but database analysis users are meant only for debug and test (no write access, no trigger/sequence creation => no delta).

 

A possible workaround is: create a transformation flow, and replicate the data into another table with CDC. then capture the changes into this table via key-hash comparison like in this blog.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/delta-processing-inside-sap-datasphere/ba-p/13954117

Then replicate the data from this table with replication flow to whatever needed.

( good luck with this "frankenstein" )

entry.title: currency in comp information portlets
entry.content:
Hi Team 

We have a requirement , where for a particular country ( Turkey) the Base pay  and other pay components are paid both in local currency and Euros.  For some employee the base pay  in Euro and other pay components are in Local currency, while for other employees the base pay in local currency and other pay components are in Euros. The Requirements is that , the compensation portlets should be displayed based on the base currency and even if the other pay components is not is as same as base currency.

For example if an employee as Base pay  in Euro and other components in Local currency, even the local currency should be displayed in Euros ( After exchange rates) , similarly if an employee as Base pay  in Local currency  and other components in Euro , even the local currency should be displayed in local currency ( After exchange rates) .

 

Just want to is it feasible to display in comp info portlets based on base pay currency

 

Regards

Kamesh Rangarajan

entry.title: Re: Delta processing inside SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Wanda_Soetomo i find probably why we can not use remote table as source.

if the remote table uses fill snapshot replica, the replication is done with tables in the same schema of the space in which the remote table is defined (as virtual table).

if the remote table uses real time replicationi (for example from an HANA source system), the replica table is inside the schema _SYS_TABLE_REPLICA.

A select statement on a virtual table with replica is routed to the table in schema _SYS_TABLE_REPLICA.

Probably there are limitations in one of those use case when it comes to transformation flows?

entry.title: How to Fix Duplicate Component Lines in IW32 , IW31
entry.content:
Issue: In TCode IW32 OR IW31 (Maintenance Order), when adding a component in the Components tab, two identical lines were created in the RESB table—one with a Storage Location (LGORT) and one without. This happened when the Storage Location field was left empty, triggering a Warning message. After filling LGORT, a duplicate line was added.

Root Cause: The issue was traced to a CHAIN in the SAPLCOMK program (Screen 2030, Module MANDATORY_FIELD_CHK). A Warning message (Application Area: EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC, Message Number: 000) caused the main loop to restart after LGORT was entered, creating a duplicate entry in RESB.

Solution:

  1. Go to TCode SM30.

  2. Enter the view V_160M_PM_1 and select Maintain.

  3. Create the message:

    • Application Area: EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC

    • Message Number: 000

    • Message Type: I (information)

  4. Save and create a Transport Request.

  5. Test in IW32 to confirm only one line is created in RESB.

Why It Works: Changing the message to Information prevents the program from restarting the loop after LGORT is filled, avoiding duplicate entries.

Tips:

  • Verify the change in a Sandbox environment with different materials and Plants.

  • Check SAP Notes for similar issues (search “SAPLCOMK V_160M_PM_1 EAM_EWM_INTEG_PROC”).

  • Debug SAPLCOMK (Screen 2030) if the issue persists.

Has anyone else faced this in SAP PM? Share your fixes or questions below!

Solution by Ebrahim Heydari

entry.title: Is possible to increase the scheduling horizon beyond the 3 numeric numbers (eg 999 maximum)
entry.content:
Hi,

Is possible to increase the scheduling horizon beyond the 3 numeric numbers (eg 999 maximum) either for Plant or MRP group or both? (Tables T399D or T438M field UMTHZ, data element UMTHZ.)

Many thanks 

Adrian

entry.title: Re: How to synchronize Set Controlling Hierarchies in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
@Martina_J timedependancies in S/4 with hierarchies are quite complex and I think this still needs improvement.

We used hierarchies in the first S/4 hana system to replicate the hierarchies, we used also own groups to replicate them. The main idea is good but it has major problems - which convinced me not to do it anymore and take another way.

1. It is not a real Fiori hierarchy and it still has to be migrated because it is not usable in Manage Global hierarchies, so you still have to migrate if Profit Center Groups or GL Account Groups are not used anymore in the backend.

2.it still depends on the technical name of the hierarchy whereever you use it in BW-Queries or analytical queries as hard values. This will lead over the time to many reports which may have to be changed manually on each report when you change to it to the real fiori hierarchies (there is a migration option but I don't think this will really help)

3. Replication is only possible for the current time slice, old time slices can only be changed when never ones are deleted. This is crucial when you find old things which have to be changed due to certain problems.

We have at the moment another solution which solves all three problems but we have other compromises:

- Synchronization must be done with a daily job so it is not automatically done. I asked SAP for the change in the SAP Std. ABAP report for this, but they're not interested to do it the same way like the replication option.

- Due to synchronization the Semantic Tags can not be used because the technical synchronization deletes the whole time slices and creates it again. If we could have a delta synchronization, which should be possible in general then we could keep the Semantic Tags. I am not sure but maybe the replication method does have the same issue. 

- Also due to synchronization in Fiori it can not be used as a reference / link to other hierarchies like the groups do. Technically it is possible but the reference is lost each time it is synchronized.

We also solved problems with older Profitcenter groups like GROUP.2024, GROUP.2023 and merged them automatically into Fiori Hierarchies, but also here SAP was not able to add this into the SAP Std. ABAP report, so we did it on our own. 

Conclusion: Our solution is more future proof, more flexible and solves issues which replication does have. But it still comes with compromises, which should be technically solvable like a delta synchronization.

entry.title: Downtime during s/4 cloud public updates + 24/7 production
entry.content:
I'm having trouble finding a definitive answer to this. My current understanding is that the typical downtime is around 4 hours, scheduled over the weekend during nighttime hours. (which timezone?)

However, what happens if you're running a 24/7 production environment? How is your operation impacted during that downtime?

  • Order Processing: No creation or updates of production orders, confirmations, etc.

  • Inventory Management: No material movement postings are possible.

  • Label Printing: Label printing is unavailable — a critical issue in highly automated environments like ours.

Additionally, how do you handle any failed messages or transactions once the system comes back online?

Hoping to learn more about this topic 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to do create with Odata V4 with Guid on Item with association

entry.title: Campaign Code

entry.title: Course Update: Exploring SAP Customer Data Platform Essentials
entry.content:
Greetings everyone! It's time to elevate your digital learning experience with our freshly updated Exploring SAP Customer Data Platform Essentials learning journey.  Whether you want to refresh your knowledge or kickstart your journey with SAP Customer Data Platform, dive right in!

Experience learning at no cost on our innovative e-learning platform, Exploring SAP Customer Data Platform Essentials. Upon completing the course and completing the associated quiz, you will be rewarded with a digital badge, a symbol of your achievement. This valuable digital emblem can be proudly displayed on your social media profiles and within your CV.

Learning objectives

 

  • Foundational Concepts: Begin with an understanding of customer data and platforms, laying the groundwork for deeper insights.

  • Console Navigation: Learn to configure and utilize the SAP Customer Data Platform effectively.

  • Four-Step Process: Dive into Data Ingestion, Identity Resolution, Insights, and Engagement to develop a comprehensive customer profile.

  • Customer Schemas and Integration: Explore the creation of custom schemas and how to integrate applications seamlessly using the Application Library.

  • Event Configuration: Gain skills in setting up events to ensure consistent data flows and integrity.

  • Insights and Engagement: Focus on generating data insights for customer classification and designing CX Journeys and Flows to enhance interactions.

 

What next?

Upon completing this self-paced course, you can explore more advanced courses to further enhance your knowledge of the SAP Customer Data Platform. Visit the following links to access additional learning journeys: Administrating SAP Customer Data Platform and Developing with SAP Customer Data Platform.

If you have the learning hub subscription you can access our Hands-on Practice for SAP Customer Data Platform Implementation Training where you can carry out exercises from the course SAP Customer Data Platform Implementation Training or to experiment on your own with Customer Data Platform.

After completing these courses, you can take out  SAP Customer Data Platform Implementation classroom training and prepare for SAP Certified Development Associate  – Implementation Consultant - SAP Customer Data Platform.

If you just want to keep yourself updated, keep checking our SAP Customer Data Platform learning page for the latest product learning offerings.

If you have any feedback or suggestions about our learning offerings, please leave a comment in this post.

Until next time, keep learning and exploring!

entry.title: Unable to split outbound delivery in S/4HANA on-premise
entry.content:
Hi

I created SO with different delivery date to create split delivery.

When the delivery date of item line 1 and 2 apart more than a week, I was able to split the delivery. On the other hand, when those two date apart less than a week, I could not split the delivery.

I used same BP and material.

If there any settings in SPRO can cause this issue?

Pratik Malasane

Please try again later." The same has been happening with every other page on that website. Would you happen to know the reason?

entry.title: Migration to RISE with SAP with Selective Data Transition: Flexibility is the Key
entry.content:
In an era where digital transformation is not just a goal but a necessity, many companies face a central question: How can we successfully move to SAP S/4HANA and position the entire organization for the future? For many, the answer lies in RISE with SAP – SAP’s comprehensive cloud offering. It combines software, infrastructure, and services in one integrated package. But the journey is complex: processes, data, and systems must be securely migrated to the new environment without operational risks.

This article explains how the Selective Data Transition (SDT) approach supports this transition – enabling companies to migrate to the cloud quickly, efficiently, and flexibly.

RISE with SAP: More Than Just Cloud Infrastructure

RISE with SAP provides businesses with a clear outlook for the future: a flexible, cloud-based ERP environment with access to cutting-edge technologies like artificial intelligence, automation, real-time analytics, and process intelligence. But it’s about more than technological innovation – RISE represents a holistic approach to business transformation.

Key benefits for companies include:

  • Flexible operating models: SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Private Edition or SAP S/4HANA On-Premise in a modular concept
  • Standardization & harmonization: Consolidate and optimize processes and systems
  • Scalable innovation: Rapid access to new features and innovations via the cloud

The goal is to pave the way for an agile, scalable, and innovation-ready ERP environment. But technological capabilities alone aren’t enough to overcome the associated challenges. This is where Selective Data Transition (SDT) comes in: a selective migration approach that combines the strengths of the traditional Greenfield and Brownfield methods – and delivers results faster.

The Challenges of Traditional Migration Approaches

When migrating to SAP S/4HANA, the how is just as important as the what. One of the main challenges is to ensure a seamless transition without disrupting ongoing operations. This forces companies to ask: Should we completely redesign the ERP system from scratch (Greenfield), or will a technical system conversion (Brownfield) suffice?

A closer look at the traditional approaches can help guide the decision:

  • Greenfield approach: This method involves a complete redesign of the system landscape. Companies have the opportunity to redefine their processes – especially useful when outdated or inefficient structures exist. However, this approach requires significant investment and involves long implementation times.
  • Brownfield approach: This involves a technical conversion of the existing system. Most processes and data are retained, allowing for faster implementation. However, it carries the risk of carrying over legacy issues that can hinder innovation. However, it carries several risks: legacy issues from previous system versions may be transferred unchanged, limiting future innovation. Moreover, since there is typically no selective data cleansing or reduction, large systems tend to remain large – leading to increased complexity, higher operational costs, and limited agility in the long run.

Beyond these two traditional paths, Selective Data Transition (SDT) has emerged as a forward-looking solution – bridging the gap between Greenfield and Brownfield and enabling companies to shape their transition to SAP S/4HANA in an innovative and sustainable way.

Selective Data Transition: A Hybrid Migration Approach

Selective Data Transition (SDT) combines the best of both worlds. But what exactly makes this approach so unique?

  • Selective data transfer: Companies can decide exactly which data and processes to carry over to the new system. This allows for outdated or redundant information to be discarded, while retaining business-critical data. At the same time, this approach enables companies to systematically migrate the data that will be relevant for future machine learning and AI applications – a crucial step toward becoming a truly data-driven enterprise. In addition, the data can be freely adopted to enable further optimizations as described below.
  • Business process optimization: Unlike pure system conversion, SDT allows for a detailed analysis and targeted improvement of existing processes. Companies can eliminate inefficient workflows and implement best practices – actively shaping their digital transformation.
  • Harmonization and consolidation: Many organizations operate multiple SAP systems in parallel – often due to mergers or international expansion. SDT enables the consolidation of various systems into one unified S/4HANA environment, simplifying the IT landscape and reducing costs.
  • Tailored to business needs: Every company has specific requirements for its IT environment. SDT enables the creation of customized migration paths that align with individual business models and structures.

This hybrid approach provides the flexibility companies need to build a sustainable, future-proof SAP landscape.

RISE with SAP and SDT: A Powerful Combination

Cloud migration is a central component of many companies’ digital strategies. A strategic move via SDT offers several key advantages:

  • Flexibility and tailored strategy: While a full Greenfield approach often implies a radical change, SDT enables a gradual transition to the cloud. Companies can align their migration roadmap with their business strategy.
  • Cost efficiency through cloud technologies: Moving to RISE with SAP allows companies to benefit from a subscription-based model instead of large one-time investments. At the same time, selective migration reduces effort and associated costs.
  • Business continuity: One major challenge of migration projects is minimizing downtime. SDT enables a controlled transfer of data and processes, allowing business operations to continue largely uninterrupted during the transition.
  • Future-proof through innovation: RISE with SAP provides access to modern cloud technologies such as AI-powered analytics, automation, and IoT integration. With SDT, companies can strategically integrate these innovations into their existing IT landscape – without risking a complete system overhaul.

Ready for the Future

The question is no longer if you should migrate to SAP S/4HANA, but how. Companies looking to leverage the full potential of the cloud should define their migration strategy now. The SDT approach offers a flexible, future-ready way to modernize IT landscapes and strategically position businesses for what lies ahead.

Those who act early will be best positioned to fully capitalize on the competitive edge offered by digital transformation.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Onboarding: Enhancements to the E-Verify Further Action Notice process
entry.content:
Hi @ThomasBilbaugh ,

Thank you for this blog, it has been very helpful in testing the new online TNC Process. I had a few questions, could you please clarify following points:

I could not find the new notification template in our system: (ONB) Confirm New HIre's Tentative Nonconfirmation Response Template. Could you please confirm if this title is correct.

Further, the revision in this blog also made on: If new hire select the information is Incorrect, the process goes back to the Hiring Manager to confirm the incorrect information. From here, the Hiring Manager can make a choice and if necessary, correct the data and potentially a new Form I-9. : Would the process be Hiring manager correct information in employee profile and then initiate Correct I-9 manually via manage data> Trigger I-9 flow and do they initiate correction first and then submit the TNC E-verify case?

entry.title: Issue with BDT Enhancement for custom fields in BUT000 in BP Tcode
entry.content:
The requirement is to add two new custom fields in BP Transaction in a new tab. We have followed BDT approach, and all the customizations are done accordingly as per below blog:-

SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement - SAP Community

The custom tab along with fields are now visible and are editable in Change/Create mode as well.

However, when we are trying to change the value in these two custom fields during change/create process in BP Tcode, the data is not getting saved at both the BP screen level and the table level (BUT000).

Step 1:

Open a BP in BP Tcode and check Additional Data tab.

 

Fields are blank currently.

2- Go to Change mode and enter some values:

 

3- After pressing save, these values are not getting saved in BUT000 table:

My PAI Code:

FUNCTION zmdg_bup01_pai.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*
DATA: false TYPE boole-boole VALUE ' ',

lt_but000 TYPE TABLE OF but000,

table_name_but000 TYPE fsbp_table_name VALUE 'BUT000'.

 

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <but000> LIKE LINE OF lt_but000.

 

CHECK cvi_bdt_adapter=>is_direct_input_active( ) = false.

* step 1: update xo memory from technical screen structure

cvi_bdt_adapter=>get_current_bp_data(

EXPORTING

i_table_name = table_name_but000

IMPORTING

e_data_table = lt_but000[]

).

 

IF lt_but000[] IS INITIAL.

IF gs_but000 IS NOT INITIAL.

gs_but000-partner = cvi_bdt_adapter=>get_current_bp( ).

APPEND gs_but000 TO lt_but000.

ENDIF.

ELSE.

READ TABLE lt_but000 ASSIGNING <but000> INDEX 1.

<but000>-zownership = gs_but000-zownership.
<but000>-zclass = gs_but000-zclass.

ENDIF.

 

cvi_bdt_adapter=>data_pai(

i_table_name = table_name_but000

i_data_new = lt_but000[]

i_validate = false

).

* step 2: check fields

CHECK cvi_bdt_adapter=>get_activity( ) <> cvi_bdt_adapter=>activity_display.

ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Re: SF Variable Pay - View Details
entry.content: Hi Ajnos,

entry.title: Re: Dynamic assignment variant for Field-symbols not allowed
entry.content: The warning is about ASSIGN (variable_containing_variable_name) TO <field_symbol>, so technically speaking it's exactly the same case. For this one, the replacement is ASSIGN instance->(variable_containing_attribute_name) TO <field_symbol>. Note that, for now, abap2xlsx contains code which compiles in all versions since ABAP 7.02, so it cannot contain the latter form, maybe in the future there will be some arbitration if ABAP loses its ascending compatibility.

entry.title: Re: CAMT.053 and CAMT.052 in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hey,

 

I guess you can use MT942 instead of the CAMT52 format. This should work on ECC as well.

 

Best,

Edrilan

entry.title: Re: API for retrieving SAP Security Notes in a machine readable format
entry.content: Hi.

entry.title: Transaktion SMX - Fehlender Löschen Button
entry.content:
Guten Tag, 

nach dem Releaseupgrade S/4 Hana 2022 SPS5 findet wir den Button "löschen" nicht mehr.
Job auf Datenbank löschen.

Kann mir jemand Hinweise und Tipps angeben?

Besten Dank

entry.title: Re: Is possible to increase the scheduling horizon beyond...
entry.content:
Hello @AdrianW 

Theoretically it is possible to modify the UMTHZ data element and enhance it. However that would lead to a massive regeneration of all dependent objects and quite possibly break the system. 

Why do you need to enhance rescheduling period? Are 3 digits not enough? That is almost 3 years!

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: It is possible to uncheck by default show groups feature in Data Analyzer?
entry.content:
Hello,

Whenever I will open Data Analyzer from script NavigationUtils.openDataAnalyzer(...); by default dimensions are grouped in the list. It is possible to have that by default unchecked? Without need to uncheck that manually and have flat presentation.

entry.title: Re: 417 An authorization problem occurred when downloading the configuration
entry.content: great job

entry.title: Re: SF Variable Pay - View Details
entry.content: Hi Ajnos, I think you will be able to rename the business section header on "Configure Label Names and Visibility screen" if only if FBP is disabled (cons: need to rebuild the FBP setup you have made after renaming business section header/s and enabling back the FBP), or please try adding this XML code below in your XML VP Template

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not supported in the selected project&quo
entry.content: You're choosing the wrong menu, it's NOT business catalog, it's Application Job Catalog Entry.

entry.title: Re: How to display the field "Trademark" in the "Manage P...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the field MARA-BRAND_ID is a standard 4-character identifier for brands. Here's how it works across the system:

  1. Display/Edit in "Manage Product Master Data" App:

    • The BRAND_ID field is not shown by default.

    • To make it visible/editable, use the "Custom Fields and Logic" app (In-App Extensibility), if the field is extensible in the app.

  2. Longer Trademark Names:

    • Since BRAND_ID only supports 4 characters, use Product Descriptions (MAKT-MAKTX) or create a custom field to store full trademark or brand names.

  3. Adding the Field in the Data Migration Template:

    • The standard "Product" migration template does not include BRAND_ID.

    • If needed, create a custom field and enable it for migration using the Custom Fields and Logic app, then regenerate the template in "Migrate Your Data".

Important: Direct editing of the MARA table isn't possible in the cloud edition;

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Method 'TIMESHEETENTRYSE_UPDATE_ENTITY' not implemented in data provider class
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using API_MANAGE_WORKFORCE_TIMESHEET to create timesheet entries in public cloud. This works fine, but the delete and the update functionality returns the same error, "Method not implemented in data provider class. I used SAP business accelerator hub to download the EDMX and upload into my service consumption model. I copied the code from the delete and update examples into my custom class, but still no luck. Any help would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Force HU-LB for Planned Replenishment
entry.content:
Hi Ajit,

only pallets are stored in the reserve storage type but the quantity can differ. Only HUs will be moved to the fixed bin storage type so there is no picking involved in the process.

Basically what we want to achieve is that only HU and storage bin is displayed and verified in RF environment while right now for the product LB we see information like product, quantity etc. which is irrelevant for our replenishment process. I know the verification fields can be changed for the REPL activity but they would still be displayed.

We dont have quantity classifications or packaging specifications.

Attached the settings:

WPT:

Receiving Storage Type:

Reserve Storage Type:

Eclipse Version: 2025-03 (4.35)
ADT: 3.50.0 (com.sap.adt.core)
[Edit:
ABAP Cleaner: 1.21.1 ]

entry.title: How to Set Facets Visibility in CDS Based on a Feature Toggle?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working with cds and have a scenario where I want to conditionally show/hide a facet based on a feature toggle. The facet should only be visible in the UI if the feature toggle is ON.

Is there a way to conditionally set field visibility in the .cds file or through annotations, based on a feature toggle? Has anyone implemented something similar and can share best practices or code snippets?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Cloud Economics (aka FinOps) with SAP BTP
entry.content: Further reading ... BTP FinOps: Keeping Track of your Credits - SAP Community

entry.title: ATC Configuration for Custom code analysis
entry.content:
Hi Team,

ATC Configuration: to include the our satellite system in to the ATC Central system for Custom code analysis. Both systems are on HANA database.

Thanks & Regards.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ATC Configuration for Custom code analysis
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for reaching out and providing the details about your ATC configuration query. I understand that you need to include your satellite system into the ATC Central System for custom code analysis.

Recommended Steps and Resources

  1. Remote Code Analysis Setup:

    • I recommend starting with the technical setup guide for configuring remote code analysis in ATC. You can find a comprehensive blog post here: Remote Code Analysis in ATC - One central check system. This guide will walk you through the steps necessary to connect your satellite system (ATS) to the central system (GS4).

  2. ATC in S/4HANA FAQs:

    • We have an extensive FAQ section on ATC in S/4HANA which might address many of your initial questions. You can access it here.

  3. Generating ATC Findings in Excel:

    • If you need to work with ATC findings in an Excel file, this guide will be helpful: Get your ATC findings into an Excel file.

  4. ATC in S/4HANA Public Cloud Configuration:

    • For specific configurations related to ATC in S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can refer to this page: ATC in S/4HANA (Public Cloud).

Identifying Object Blacklist & Whitelist and Relevant SAP Notes

To identify the object blacklists and whitelists and relevant SAP notes for including the satellite system (ATS) into the central system (GS4), you can follow these steps:

  1. Object Blacklist & Whitelist:

    • Refer to the SAP Community for recommendations on managing object blacklists and whitelists. You can also find specific details in the ATC in S/4HANA FAQs.

  2. Relevant SAP Notes:

    • SAP Notes for ATC configuration are regularly updated. You can search for the latest SAP notes on the SAP Support Portal using keywords like "ATC S/4HANA configuration" and "ATS integration".

I hope this information will help you with smooth integration of your satellite system into the central ATC system.

Kind Regards,
Luiza
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Cloud Economics (aka FinOps) with SAP BTP
entry.content: Furhter further reading ... SAP Discovery Center Mission - Control your Operational Cost: Setting up a BTP Financial Operations application

entry.title: Discrepancy in planned effort in "Plan Customer Projects" and C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We see some discrepancy in planned effort of Work Package and planned effort value in C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2  query.

For example, planned effort for month of May 2025 is 100 hours but in the query we are having the value as 90 hours.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Discrepancy in planned effort in "Plan Customer Proje...
entry.content:
Hello,

The 90 Hours for Jan 2025 is coming from the Effort (Staffed) rather than Effort (Planned). This is the intended behaviour for C_EngmntProjFcstQuery_2 when there is a Staffed Effort. Please review the distribution under Effort (Staffed) in Plan Customer Projects to see where the values are being pulled from.

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: WBS ELEMENT RELEASE WITH WORKFLOW
entry.content:
Dear @Prasenjit123 ,

for enterprise project the app "manage workflows for enterprise projects" (F6158) is available for handling a release approval workflow. You can find additional information regarding workflows for projects here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/d2f49cef25ae4b2e8e00b3491e5224c8.html?locale=en-US 

KR

Janina

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ Training by SAP
entry.content: ok thank you @Margit_Wagner

entry.title: solution for Personnel Cost Planning
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,

We are looking for a solution for Personnel Cost Planning.

Please let me know about the solution that SAP offers for this.

Regards,

Jina

entry.title: Re: when posting using 543 movement then sys is consideri...
entry.content:
Hello @SAP10001 

The 543 movement type is consumption of components provided to a subcontractor. If 543 is valuated with the standard price, it means that the consumption is from the valuation type B/D.

If you want to consider the moving average price, 543 needs to post from the valuation type R.

You can determine valuation types automatically with a stock determination strategy.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Track Quality and Compliance with SAP Digital Manufacturing Reports
entry.content:
Knowing what happened on the shop floor and why is key to running a reliable, high-quality operation.

SAP Digital Manufacturing makes this easy with standard reports, including SFC and Order Reports, which can give you a clear, detailed view of each product's journey through production, including any issues that came up and how they were handled. It’s everything you need to drive quality, meet compliance, and keep improving.

Here are some of the benefits of the standard reports, in SAP Digital Manufacturing:

📋 Complete Production History
Get a full view of each SFC’s lifecycle, including steps, timestamps, and outcomes.

❌ Nonconformance Visibility
Easily track where and why issues occurred, helping drive quick resolution and root cause analysis.

🔍 End-to-End Traceability
Follow materials, components, and tools used at each step for compliance and quality audits.

📊 Data-Driven Decisions
Use detailed reports to identify trends, improve processes, and reduce repeat issues.

✅ Regulatory and Customer Compliance
Meet traceability requirements with clear, accessible documentation of production and quality events.

Learn more at Nonconformances Processes within SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

On 24th October 2024, I had applied for Free SAP Certificate and email sent to digitalskills@sap.com from my email address muhammad_ghous@hotmail.com within the deadline but I have not received any reply or feedback from your side:

Can you please look this.

Best regards,

Ghous

entry.title: Datasphere & SAC Users and Activities Source Tables
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to create usage reports (Activities related to which views from Datasphere are accessed by SAC and the user that viewed the story in SAC and last login date for individual users for both Datasphere and SAC) based on the Users and Activities tables found in Datasphere and SAC under the Security Tab.

 

I would like to avoid having to download the CSVs from the User and Activities tabs completely and rather use the underlying tables where this data is stored directly.

Where can I find the source tables? Using the Database Explorer on Datasphere I have looked under the SYS and DWC_Global schemas so far with no luck.

entry.title: Re: Moving Q-stock between Plants
entry.content:
If Inspection lot created in one plant for quality stock then its not possible to move it wihtout completing the UD for it. You can complete the inspection in first plant itself. while posting the UD you can move the material to restricted use or stock transfer storage location in QA32 itself.

Later move the material to another plant with 322 movement. Also activate the 08 transfer posting inspection in second plant with same inspection plan. So the stock will be moved to Quality with inspection lot. You can record the Inspection results over there. But its double time activity.

You can use app transfer stock cross plant.

entry.title: Re: DELETE option is not there to delete an employee unde...
entry.content: Ok. Users existing in SuccessFactors Platform should be managed through BizX/Platform, including their deletion/purge (KBA 2615360).

entry.title: Re: Question about replication flow in initial mode with...
entry.content: you should also check report R_JR_UTIL_1. Check option "Show Job Repository State", if it is inactiv, activate it with "Change Job Repository State" and after that trigger the Data/Replication Flow from your DSP

entry.title: Re: Users Cannot See Their Own Contingent Worker Profile...
entry.content:
Update:

I’ve confirmed that the Employment Switcher is available in our Production instance, but not in the Demo environment.

That’s good enough for me at this point — I won’t be raising a support ticket.

Thanks to everyone who contributed. I’ll go ahead and close this post now.

entry.title: Re: Cube compression WITH zero elimination option
entry.content: Report SAP_NULL_ELIMINATION The table with the active data contains records for which all key figures are equal to 0. You can use this report to delete such records

entry.title: Certification
entry.content:
hi,

I wanted to know how valid and useful can a SAP certificate from a random coaching institute be as a fresher. Will it be accepted by companies?

entry.title: Re: Publishing to a Queue or Topic in BTP Event Mesh usin...
entry.content: ㄹㅇㄴㅁㄹㅇㄴㅁ

entry.title: Re: Publishing to a Queue or Topic in BTP Event Mesh usin...
entry.content: Dear Tobias_Griebe. Thanks to your information and additional tests, I got the results I wanted. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Exclude blocked (or marked for deletion) customers fr...
entry.content: There is no standard provision to address this. As suggested, you can consider user exit but instead of hard coding the Ship-Tos, what you can do is to add such of those Ship-Tos in TVARVC table along with order type or document type. With this, via mv45afzz, as and when new sale orders are created, system, in the backend, should cross check this table and if the Sold-To combination is same, then, should hide the corresponding Ship-Tos. By doing so, even the Business users can add more Ship-Tos via SM30 and no need to depend on ABAP team for code change

entry.title: Re: BTP Cockpit - ABAP trial instance creation issue
entry.content: Thanks for your reply, created new ABAP instance now.

entry.title: Re: Changing the column headers of a custom database table
entry.content: Create your own data elements as copy of the used ones. Use the new data elements in your table definition. Give your own description to your data elements.

entry.title: Re: Hide People selector button in Goals. release 2025
entry.content: Hi.Is it possible to remove or translate this button?

entry.title: Re: BPM app CALM "Host Name"
entry.content:
In order to be able to mark this question as answered, moving the solution to answer from comment:

Hi Rayane,

Are you looking for the hostname of your SAP Cloud ALM tenant? 

As per your screenshot, you can find it in SAP Cloud ALM service key. If your tenant was provisioned before June 2023 then, you will have to download it from your SAP Cloud ALM subaccount via SAP BTP Cockpit. Otherwise, you can view/download from Landscape Management app in SAP CLoud ALM itself.

Please refer to this link for details and steps - https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/integration-monitoring/calm-co...

Hope this helps. In case if this doesn't solve your issue or the issue described is different then please do elaborate.

Regards

Shubhra

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Cross-Billing Cycle Aggregation for Tier-Based Pricing in SAP Subscription Bill
entry.content: This configuration is also described in the Pricing Configuration Guide.

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not supported in the selected project&quo
entry.content: I have created Application Job Catalog and Job template as well. I am unable to see custom Job templates in the Maintain Application Jobs screen after clicking on create option and drop down. Also, I created custom template from Fiori App - Application Job Templates, still I don't see it in Application jobs screen. How do I get custom Job template in Application jobs screen? Any inputs?

entry.title: Re: How to open ObjectPage in edit mode in odata V4
entry.content:
I created a sample how to do it here:

https://github.com/gregorwolf/bookshop-demo/pull/123/files

sap.ui.define(
    ['sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension'],
    function (ControllerExtension) {
      'use strict';
  
      return ControllerExtension.extend('orders.ext.ObjectPageExt', {
        override: {
          routing: {
            // Method to handle the binding context set up after navigation to this view.
            onAfterBinding: function (bindingContext) {
              // Update the button state based on the new binding context.
              // Get the current binding context of the view.
              const bindingContext2 = this.base.getView().getBindingContext();
              if(bindingContext.getBinding().getPath().startsWith("/Orders")){
                bindingContext2.requestObject().then(
                  function (dataObject) {
                    this.base.editFlow.editDocument(bindingContext2)
                  }.bind(this)
                );
              }
              },
          }
        }
      });
    } 
  );

entry.title: Re: Using Connector Builder in Open Connectors - Mailchimp Tutorial
entry.content:
Very great article, helped very much!

What I'm missing is: how can I integrate the Webhooks into my Integration Suite iFlows. You can't set an OpenConnector as a message start Trigger - so how can I get the webhook data?

entry.title: Re: Define Search String for Electronic Bank Statement in Cloud?
entry.content: Latest documentation on Manage Processing Rules for Bank Statements is available here: Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/d6fe6526b26f46e59d9d1dd0f7427fe3.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Purchasing Document by Sales Order Number
entry.content: Table EKKN is another table you can pull POs by SOs

entry.title: Reporting on completed workflows
entry.content:
Hi,
We have set up Custom MDF's where there are the following participants of a workflow:
Initiator: The manager initiates a workflow when adding the MDF to his employee.
Approver: This goes to a seperate approver
Employee: The employee is the final step. He/she checks the workflow and approves if all is OK.

That all works as expected of course, but when these workflows are completed, we have a need of picking them up and archiving them. Normally I would just read the data of the MDF from Odata API on custom MDF object of user. But I also need to fetch information about the approvers in the workflow, and at what date they signed/approved the workflow.

I was hoping to be able to do this with the latest release of 1H 2025, but cant seem to figure out how to fetch the participants, and date of approvals.

With only one initiator and one final approver, I can store the user and date in the MDF itself, as the creation of the MDF is triggering a save (so I can populate hidden fields) and the final accept of the workflow is also triggering a save (so I can populate final details). But with an approver in the middle, not changing anything (only approving or sending back), there is no save action, so no way to pick up the user and date of this "middle" approver. Hence the need to access this from API after workflow is completed.

Any tips on this would be great!

See image for reference. This is what I can see when browsing completed workflows from the "Workflow Requests Page". I want to fetch Initiator, Approver and Employee (final approver) as shown here, with userID/name and date of approval.

entry.title: What are all the standard integration option SAP PM (ECC) has with Oracle Cloud Fusion?
entry.content:
Dear Gurus. 

Please help me understand all the standard integration option SAP ECC Plant Maintenance has with Oracle Cloud Fusion (Logistics, Finance and HR).

Your guidance will greatly help me be better prepared for my organization's upgradation from ECC to S4HANA.

Thanks.

entry.title: Express Error During Insert into EINA – Key
entry.content:
Hello,

Some users have encountered the following express error: "Error during insert EINA with key 5300047395"

Initially, I investigated the most common cause—number range inconsistencies—but I verified that the number ranges in our system are correctly maintained.
I have also checked some Notes: 1955849 and 1906905 but there are already implemented in our system.

What could caused that and how can i resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hi Matija,

I'm both using the shortcut Shift + F2 and manually using Tools > Queries > User Queries each time to see if any of those options returns any value.

Regards, 

Santiago

entry.title: Re: SAP DBTech JDBC: [1034]: exceed maximum number of ext...
entry.content:
This doesn't look like an out of memory error to me.

From the stack trace, HikariCP is attempting to create a new connection and the server is responding with "SAP DBTech JDBC: [1034]: exceed maximum number of external connections".

I suggest reducing the maximum size of the connection pool in Hikari.

entry.title: Re: Is possible to increase the scheduling horizon beyond...
entry.content:
Hi @DominikTylczyn 

Many thanks for your reply.

We are in the manufactoring industry and have some production items that can take up to 5 years to produce! (Beyond the limit of 999 days)

Fully appreciate that enhancing the data element could have a greater impact on the system than we would wish for.

Are there any BADIs that could adjust the scheduling horizon dynamically?

Thanks Adrian

entry.title: Recuperar mi usuario S...
entry.content: Buen día, en mi cuenta tengo varios usuarios S por motivos laborales, pero en uno de ellos llego a su fecha de vencimiento y por consiguiente no he podido entrar con el y los estudios que estoy haciendo al parecer el sistema los esta llevando con esa cuenta en especial (he entrado con los otros usuarios S y lo que he estudiado no aparece), es posible recuperar ese usuario y lo mas importante que la información de lo que estoy haciendo en el Hub Learning no se pierda?

entry.title: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio

Today, we’re excited to share more details about Joule Studio, a new capability in SAP Build designed specifically for creating custom AI agents and Joule skills powered by Agentic AI. With Joule Studio, you can easily design, deploy, and manage intelligent agents uniquely tailored to your organization’s processes and data. It’s a significant step forward in enabling you to apply AI to tackle real-world challenges, streamline operations, and deliver tangible business impact.

“Joule Studio has helped us optimize resource allocation, improve time management, and enhance collaboration by using Agentic AI for our project management workflows. We have also used Joule Studio to enhance our AGIL AI.TimeManager application for time and activity management.”  
- Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience Services, AGILITA Switzerland / Germany 

What is Joule Studio?

Traditional automation often falls short of addressing the complexity and dynamism of modern enterprises. While agents can think, reason, and adapt like skilled team members, they typically struggle with understanding business context and data, limiting their effectiveness across diverse landscapes.

Joule Studio, a capability of SAP Build, enables you to create purpose-built Joule agents and skills. It brings together SAP’s business process expertise and data from its comprehensive set of Cloud ERP applications with Agentic AI. Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph, these agents gain semantic access to business-relevant information across both SAP and non-SAP applications, empowering them to perform their tasks with greater context and precision. As part of SAP Build, agents can also interact with workflows, automations, and custom applications, and be embedded into your own solutions to extend and enhance business processes.

Here’s what you can build in Joule Studio with its drag-and-drop simplicity:

  • AI Agents that are capable of planning, reasoning, and executing complex business scenarios. Imagine an agent optimizing your logistics network, providing risk mitigation strategies, making procurement recommendations, and sourcing candidates for interviews—all while adapting to evolving requirements.
  • Joule Skills that are designed to handle repetitive, rule-based tasks, freeing your teams from routine activities such as data entry, IT ticket raising, and PO creation, thereby boosting productivity.

Both agents and skills are seamlessly orchestrated and triggered based on user intent and the context of conversations within Joule Copilot, delivering an intuitive end-user experience.


Image 1: Create an AI agent and Joule Skill project in SAP Build


Deeply Grounded in Business Process and Data: The SAP Advantage

One of the biggest challenges in enterprise AI is grounding agents in the context of your business. Many agents fail because they lack access to the correct data and don’t understand the nuances of your business. AI agents built with Joule Studio are deeply grounded in your business:

  • Data Grounding: Agents are anchored in your unique enterprise knowledge through SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph. This ensures they understand not just the ‘what’ but also the ‘why’ behind every task, leading to more reliable outcomes.
  • Process Integration: Easily embed existing workflows, automations, integrations, and business logic from SAP Build into your agents and skills, streamlining cross-functional workflows.
  • Role Specialization: Tailor agents for specific business areas and roles, whether it’s finance, HR, supply chain, or customer service, so they align closely to your business needs.


Image 2: Provide instructions to the agent in natural language to define its goal and role


Open and Extensible Framework

Enterprises operate across multiple platforms and technologies. Joule Studio is designed to be open and extensible, allowing your AI agents to function across heterogeneous landscapes:

  • Connect to all your systems: Integrate your agents with external systems, APIs, and AI models, extending their reach beyond the SAP ecosystem. For instance, an agent could pull supply chain risk data from a third-party provider, analyze it, and offer supplier recommendations for procurement.
  • API Management and Governance: Use SAP Integration Suite to compose, govern, and monitor APIs exposed to agents and skills, including real-time usage analytics, anomaly detection, and predictive insights.
  • Flexible Model Choice: Choose from a range of out-of-the-box large language models or bring your own, ensuring your agents have the right reasoning and planning capabilities for your unique needs.


Image 3: Select from your existing action projects in SAP Build to connect to third-party systems

“Joule Studio in SAP Build is enabling teams to enrich Joule with client-specific skills and AI agents, seamlessly integrating it with the client’s application ecosystem, partner solutions, and agent network. This makes Joule a go-to solution for maximizing value and delivering a unified, agentic user experience.” 
- Stephanie Guimbellot, Global SAP Innovation & Sustainability Lead at Accenture 

Built-in Security and Compliance

Trust, transparency, and compliance are essential for AI agents. Joule Studio is built with these principles at its core:

  • Transparency: Gain complete visibility into agent planning, decision paths, and execution flows, so you can understand not just what your agents did, but why.
  • Centralized Lifecycle Management: Manage, deploy, and maintain all your agents and skills centrally within SAP Build, streamlining operations and updates.
  • Test and Validation: Test new agents and skills before deploying them into production, ensuring quality, reliability, and alignment with your business policies.
  • Compliance by Design: Ensure compliant interactions with AI using built-in data privacy controls and enforcement mechanisms.


Image 4: Test your agents and skills before deploying to minimize runtime errors.

Get a Closer Look at Joule Studio

Harness Agentic AI for your business with Joule Studio, empowering your teams to create AI agents and skills that accelerate innovation, boost productivity, and enhance decision-making. Custom Joule Skills will be generally available in June 2025 and custom AI Agents later in the year.


Want to learn more?

  • Register for the webinar: https://url.sap/2iuj2p
  • Take a guided product tour: https://url.sap/product-tour
  • Learn how to build Joule skills with this Interactive Value Journey: https://url.sap/skilldesign

entry.title: Stop automatic PR/PO conversion for MRP controllers
entry.content:
Hi community,

we would like to stop the automatic PR to PO conversion which is contolled by MRP run, but only for a specific range of MRP controllers. Can you help us?

Thanks/BR Zoran

entry.title: Re: Quering document lines in user define values
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you make sure that the form you pull data from is open and the matrix is populated with data before running the query? Does your query window pull data from the matrix? Maybe it would be best if you try it now and post a screenshot when it gives you the error (blank out any sensitive data).

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
The Authorization Concept Terms and Elements

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/exploring-the-authorization-concept-for-sap-s-4hana-and-sap-business-suite/explaining-elements-and-terminology-of-the-abap-authorization-concept

 

Authorization object class: A logical grouping of authorization objects (for example, all authorization objects for object class FI beginning with "F_").

Authorization object: Groups of 1 to 10 authorization fields together. These fields are then checked simultaneously (example: F_LFA1_APP, vendor: application authorization).

Authorization field: The smallest unit against which a check is to be run (ACTVT, APPKZ).

Authorization: An instance of an authorization object, that is, a combination of allowed values for each authorization field of an authorization object.

Authorization profile: Contains instances (authorizations) for different authorization objects.

Role: Generated using Role Maintenance (transaction PFCG), and allows the automatic generation of an authorization profile. A role describes the activities of an SAP user.

User/user master record: Used for logging on to SAP systems and grants restricted access to functions and objects of the SAP system based on authorization profiles.

Naming conventions for customer developments (see SAP Notes 20643 and 16466😞

  • Authorizations and authorization profiles are Customizing objects and must therefore not be in the customer namespace (Y, Z). They must not include an underscore in the second position.

  • Authorization classes, objects, and fields are development objects and must begin with Y or Z (customer namespace).

 

 

 

You can define several different authorizations for an authorization object. This means that an authorization object has various instances.

Example: Authorization object F_BKPF_BUK has the following authorizations:

  • Work center 1: Authorized to create, change and display documents in company code 2000.

  • Work center 2: Authorized to create, change and display documents in company code 1000.

  • Work center 3: Authorized to display documents in company code 1000.

You can assign multiple authorizations to a work center. Grouped together, these authorizations are called an authorization profile.

Example: Work center 2 has the following authorization profile:

  • Authorization to execute transaction code FB02 and FB03.

  • Authorization to create, change, and display documents in company code 1000.

  • Authorization to create, change, and display documents in business area 2000.

  • Authorization to change and display document items for the accounts receivable account type.

 

To provide users with user-specific menus after they have logged on to an SAP system, you use roles. These are defined using Role Maintenance.

A role is a set of functions, also known as activities, describing a specific work area. The "Accounts Receivable Accountant" role, for example, contains transactions, reports, and/or Internet/Intranet links that an accountant needs for his or her daily work.

In the role, you organize transactions, reports, or Web addresses in a role menu.

A large number of roles (>1200) are delivered with the standard SAP R/3 System. Before you define your own roles, check if one of the user roles delivered as part of the standard SAP R/3 System can be used.

 

The new SAP Easy Access menu provides a user-specific point of entry into the SAP system.

The user menu (created from multiple role menus) contains only those transactions, reports, and Web addresses needed by the users for their daily work processes.

The user menus can be and are often created with Role Maintenance using composite roles.

For users with system administrator authorization, the SAP Easy Access menu provides some additional functions for:

  • Creating roles

  • Calling menus for roles and assigning them to users

To use these extended functions, you need authorizations for the following authorization objects:

authorization objectValue
S_USER_TCDPFCG
S_USER_PRO*
S_USER_AUT*
S_USER_GRP*

entry.title: Re: FTI/WEBGUI_MSG123 delete default account assignment
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Create Task for Warehouse request ByDesign using SiteLogisticsTaskBundleMaintainRequest_sync_V1

This entry is wrong, because the from cost element is empty. We can't delete this wrong entry because a error message that the entry was made by SAP.

entry.title: Re: materialization is failing with error message interna...
entry.content: How are you materializing DR? What release of ASE are you using?

entry.title: Sunset of BEx Tools in SAP BW - What You Need to Know Before October 2025
entry.content:
Introduction: Sunset of BEx Tools - A Critical Milestone by October 2025

With the clock ticking toward October 14, 2025, the end of support for Microsoft Office 2016 and 2019 also signals the end of support for Excel-based SAP BEx tools like BEx Analyzer, BEx Workbooks, and BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation) by SAP. While some BEx components (BEx Query Designer, BEx WAD) will continue in maintenance mode until 2027 or 2030, the Excel-dependent tools face a hard stop, leaving many customers exposed if no action is taken.

While many organizations are already moving forward with SAP Datasphere adoption or planning their SAP BW/4HANA conversion, those still operating on SAP BW 7.x must urgently assess their current BEx tool usage to avoid disruptions and ensure long-term supportability.

In this article, we break down:

  • What BEx tools are affected and when
  • How to check if your system still relies on BEx Tools approaching end of support
  • Available replacements like SAP Analysis for Office and SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP's mass conversion tools and services to ease your transition

Whether you have already started migrating or are still evaluating your next steps, this article will help you understand the risks, available options with exisitng BEx tools usage.

BEx Solutions Overview

 SAP offers a range of BEx-related tools and components, each serving different roles in the reporting and analysis workflow. These solutions fall into two broad categories; those dependent on Microsoft Excel and those that are web-based or standalone.

BEx tools can be classified in two broader categories:

  • Excel-Dependent (Impacted by both BEx sunset and Office lifecycle)
    • BEx Analyzer
    • BEx Workbooks
    • BEx Broadcaster
  • Standalone/Web-Based (Independent of Office support)
    • BEx Query Designer
    • BEx Queries
    • BEx Web Application Designer

BEx tools that rely on Excel only work with specific versions of Microsoft Office. The last versions supported by BEx tools are Office 2016 and Office 2019. Since Microsoft's decision to stop supporting Office 2016 & 2019 versions by October 14, 2025, this poses a hard limit on the support timelines for the excel based BEx reporting tools as well, and after that BEx tools dependent on Excel will no longer be supported too by SAP.

BEx tools that don’t rely on Microsoft Office (i.e. BEx Query Designer, BEx Queries, BEx WAD) can still be used and supported until 2027 (standard support) or 2030 (extended support), in line with BW 7.50 maintenance timelines. These tools are now in maintenance-only mode, meaning SAP will only provide basic support for fixing issues in BW 7.5 with no new development, innovation or new features planned for these BEx tools. However, BEx Analyzer, BEx Workbooks, and BEx Broadcaster are dependent on Microsoft Office, so they can only be supported until October 14, 2025, when Microsoft stops supporting Office 2016 and 2019.

BEx Tools – Supportability Overview

Following table provides the End of Support for the BEx related products based on SAP NetWeaver or BEx End of Life timelines:

 Key References and Support Notes for BEx Transition

The End of support for BEx (excel based tools) is reinforced by external dependencies, such as Microsoft’s end of support for Office 2016 and 2019, which directly impacts the usability and security of BEx Analyzer and Workbooks and BEx Broadcaster.

SAP has formally communicated the BEx sunset through multiple channels, including official SAP Notes and blogs dating back as early as 2016. The references below serve as key evidence points supporting the urgency and inevitability of moving to modern alternatives like SAP Analysis for Office (AO) or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC):

  • Microsoft Support link - Microsoft will no longer provide technical support, bug fixes, or security fixes for Office 2016 and Office 2019 vulnerabilities which may be subsequently reported or discovered. This includes security updates which can help protect your PC from harmful viruses, spyware, and other malicious software
  • Announcements for BEx sunset was announced through the blog -  Announced in 2016
  • SAP Note 2609031 - Sunset of BW Business Explorer (BEx) – Announced in 2018

Additional References:

  • SAP Note 1973324 - BEx support for Microsoft Office 365 - Client Excel 2013 and  2016
  • SAP Note 3474912 – BEx Query Designer support for BW 7.50
  • SAP Note 3590297 - SAP BW NetWeaver Cloud: Information about maintenance availability in private cloud editions
  • SAP Note 1995532 - BI 7.x tools - HW\SW requirements and Compatibility of Microsoft Operating Systems and Office Products

BEx Sunset Adoption Gaps 

As part of SAP’s long-term strategy to modernize its business intelligence tooling landscape, the sunset of BEx (Business Explorer) tools has been formally announced, encouraging customers to transition to more advanced and supported alternatives such as SAP Analysis for Office (AO) and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

However, many SAP BW 7.x customers are at varying stages of adoption readiness, and significant gaps remain in fully realizing this transition. We observed these customers in following categories:

  • Transition Planned but BEx Still in Use: BW 7.x customers who have already planned the transition from BEx tools to modern alternatives such as SAP Analysis for Office (AO) or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
    • Users have been informed about the BEx sunset through communication efforts like end-user trainings, replacement solutions and awareness sessions
    • Despite these efforts, end users continue to use BEx tools (e.g., BEx Analyzer, BEx Workbooks) for their reporting activities
  • Unaware of BEx Sunset: BW 7.x customers who are not aware of the sunset of BEx tools.
    • BEx continues to be the primary reporting tool for all business use cases, as users are not informed of alternative solutions or the transition roadmap

What SAP Products are impacted: 

  • SAP BW 7.5 or lower: BEx Analyzer/Workbooks can be the primary reporting front-end for classic BW systems
  • Embedded BW (in SAP ECC or ERP): Even though Embedded BW is typically used for operational reporting, BEx Analyzer/Workbooks can still used in some implementations
  • SAP SCM (Supply Chain Management): SCM systems with APO or SNP modules often have their own BW instance or rely on Embedded BW

While BEx is closely associated with SAP BW, its footprint extends into nearly every corner of the SAP ecosystem i.e. from legacy CRM and SRM systems to industry-specific solutions like IS-U and IS-Retail. Any system with a BW component or an integration to BW for analytics could potentially be generating BEx usage worth monitoring.

How to identify the impact in SAP BW system:

There are few potential options to check it in the system or EWA if BEx Analyzer or BEx Broadcaster is still used in the SAP BW system:

Option 1:

The SAP EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) report is a proactive monitoring tool provided by SAP that delivers a comprehensive technical health check of your SAP system. It is part of SAP’s support offering and is typically generated weekly to help detect and prevent potential issues before they impact system performance or availability.

Check section “Frontend Distribution” in BW EarlyWatch Alert reports. This section provides an overview of BW query executions, segmented by frontend tools, for the period covered in the EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) report. The data is sourced from the RSDDSTAT_OLAP view in the BW system.

The availability and data for the section depends on the configuration of the EarlyWatch Alert report for BW system: e.g.

ACTION REQUIRED:  If there are any entries in BEx Analyzer 7.x and/or BEx Broadcaster, it is critical to initiate remediation steps to mitigate the impact of BEx End of support

Note: The statistics in EarlyWatch Alert report are extracted from the RSDDSTAT_OLAP view. Accuracy of the data relies on the proper enablement and execution of detailed statistics collection through transaction RSDDSTAT in SAP BW system, which must be activated for Workbooks, Queries, and InfoProviders to capture the statistics.

Option 2:

If EarlyWatch alert is not activated, you can login to the BW Production system, and manually check BEx tool usage accessing transaction SE16, and table RSDDSTAT_OLAP:

  • For BEx Analyzer / Workbook usage:
    • Use selection “BEX” as STEPTP field and “3200”  as EVENTID.
    • Number of entries > 0 show the usage of BEx Analyzer / Workbooks in the system.
  • For BEx Broadcaster usage:
    • Use “14601”, “14611” and “14671” as EVENTID.
    • Number of entries > 0 show the usage of BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calc server) in the system.
  • The USERID field in the results will identify which users are actively using BEx tools

ACTION REQUIRED:  If there are any entries in either of the above cases, it is critical to initiate remediation steps to mitigate the impact of BEx end of support.

Additional Consideration: This analysis is not limited to standalone BW systems. You can also perform this check in SAP systems with the SAP_BW component, such as ECC, SCM, or CRM etc. While most customers leverage standalone BW for central reporting, embedded BW capabilities in source systems allow the creation and use of BEx queries locally; another area that must be assessed during BEx deprecation planning.

How can SAP Services help?

BEx Workbooks

To ensure continuity and supportability, all BEx Workbooks should be migrated to SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AO). For customers with only a few BEx Workbooks, a manual migration may be more practical.

For customers with a large number of BEx Workbooks, SAP offers a tool for Automated Mass Conversion Tool to accelerate and facilitate the conversion process from BEx (Analyzer) Workbooks to Analysis for Office Workbooks. The Automated Mass Conversion Tool is 6x to 8x faster than the manual conversion method, conversion of 100 Workbooks take around 5-6 Minutes, compared to at least 30 seconds per one workbook in case of manual conversion.

This service is available for both MaxAttention (Premium Engagement) and Professional Services customers, if you would like to request it or find out more about it please contact your Engagement Architect, Technical Quality Manager, Account Manager or you can reach out to us via the email:

  • SAP PremiumHubCoE_BExtoAO_Migration

BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation)

Unlike BEx Workbooks, which benefit from SAP’s Mass Conversion Tool for migration to Analysis for Office, BEx Broadcaster (Pre-calculation) presents a different challenge. There are following replacement options available:

  • SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (AO) with Scheduling via BI Platform
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Publication

There is no automated conversion tool available for migrating broadcasting scenarios to replacement alternatives. Customers must manually rebuild scheduling and distribution logic from scratch using supported replacement options.

Conclusion

The sunset of BEx tools marks the end of an era. While many customers have already transitioned to modern alternatives, there are customers who still rely on BEx tools particularly BEx Workbooks and Broadcaster for critical reporting and scheduling needs. With the hard deadline of October 14’ 2025 fast approaching, it is essential for customers to take care of their current BEx usage across the SAP ecosystem, especially within SAP BW environments.

Organizations with a significant footprint of BEx Workbooks should be aware that migrating to SAP Analysis for Office can require substantial time and manual effort. SAP offers a Mass Conversion Tool and expert services through SAP MaxAttention and the CoE teams to accelerate this process and reduce the operational burden. Please connect at SAP PremiumHubCoE_BExtoAO_Migration.

For those using BEx Broadcaster (Pre-Calculation Server), it is equally important to plan a transition to supported scheduling solutions using manual rebuild such as SAP BusinessObjects BI Scheduling or SAP Analytics Cloud Publications.

Taking proactive steps today will ensure business continuity, reduce security and compliance risks, and set your organization on a clear path toward a modern, supported, and future-ready analytics strategy beyond BEx.

entry.title: Re: Ekko-procstat valueis different when PO is released v...
entry.content:
Dear,

Chech SAP Note 3585478 (released 10.04.2025).

We had the same issue when BAPI_PO_RELEASE was called. After importing the note, ekko-procstat is changing again (03 -> 05).

entry.title: Cloud Connector access to AL11 directory
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help to understand how to get the internal host of SFTP?

--Symptom--

  • We created few folders in AL11 directory, now we need to fetch the data from that folder to CPI via cloud connector to S4HANA Private Cloud. 
  • The blog is followed:
    https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/step-by-step-procedure-to-establish-cpi-to-sftp-server-connection/ba-p/13520842

 

For Cloud Integration to access file storage, you must expose the file storage using FTP or SFTP protocol, by setting up FTP or SFTP service (as Server).

But how to get the internal host of SFTP?

Thank you very much.

entry.title: How to Add Calculated Measures from Two Different Models in SAC Story
entry.content:
Hi,

I’m working on a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Story, and I have the following requirement:

I have two calculated measures, each coming from a different analytical model (1  From Datasphere and other third Party Tools), and I’d like to create a new calculated measure by adding them together to use in a chart or KPI in the story.

Example:

  • From Model A, I have a calculated measure: Online Sales Revenue

  • From Model B, I have another calculated measure: Retail Store Sales Revenue

I want to create a new measure like:

Total Sales Revenue = Online Sales Revenue + Retail Store Sales Revenue

and use this total in a Combined KPI tile or chart within the same story.

How can we  Achieve this in SAC Story .

entry.title: Re: PowerDesigner NODE.JS update
entry.content: Hi Arnaud, version 18.20.8 is obsolete too. What should PD Web users do in such case?

entry.title: Modify the field service item in the tab of creation of service call in sap fsm
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please tell me how to put a default value for the field service item in the tab to create a service call ?

I tried to d it using screen config but it did not work

 

thank you,

entry.title: Re: Vulnerabilities and Compromises
entry.content: I go an e-mail "A priority 'Very High' SAP Note/KBA has just been released on component EP-VC-INF" (Fr 25.04.2025 14:12)

entry.title: How to send notification email to receiver in Flexible WF
entry.content:
Hi Guys

From here, How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions | SAP Help Portal,

i did predelivery template copy of SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS for purchase requisition 

and setup flexible WF for purchase requisition. but i can trigger workflow but cannot get notification email. 

Who know which configuration i miss?

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP Business Suite Post-Sapphire Update: What Partners Must Know
entry.content:
The SAP purpose has always been clear: to help the world run better and improve people’s lives. In today’s world of increasing complexity and change, this purpose is more relevant than ever. SAP Business Suite empowers partners and customers to execute on this vision by recognizing the unprecedented challenges businesses face — and by delivering intelligent, interconnected technologies that help them not only adapt but thrive.

That’s why we’re inviting all SAP Partners to a dedicated enablement session designed to help you get up to speed on the latest updates coming out of SAP Sapphire.

Enablement Session for SAP Partners
SAP Business Suite – Post-Sapphire Update
Date: June 4–5
Audience: SAP Partners only

What to Expect:
✔ Recap of the SAP Business Suite launch at SAP Sapphire
✔ Updated go-to-market messaging tailored for partner conversations
✔ Key pricing and commercial changes
✔ Live Q&A with SAP Business Suite product marketing experts

This is your opportunity to ask questions, sharpen your positioning, and walk away with actionable insights to better support your customers.

REGISTER NOW
